title: Class
description: Schema for class JSON files that define classes in the model. Class files are located within domain directories (e.g., 'order_management/order.class.json'). The class key is constructed from the domain key prefix plus the filename: for 'order_management/order.class.json', the key is 'order_management.order'. Classes represent entities with attributes and behavior. If validation fails, verify 'name' is present and non-empty, and check that any referenced keys (actor_key, attribute keys in indexes) exist.

name.description: The display name of the class (e.g., 'Order', 'Customer Account'). This is the human-readable name shown in diagrams. Must be non-empty. If validation fails: ensure this is a string with at least one character.

details.description: Optional detailed description of the class. Describe what entity this class represents, its purpose in the domain, and any important business rules or constraints. Helps readers understand the class's role in the model.

actor_key.description: Optional identity subkey referencing the actor that owns or primarily interacts with this class. Must match an actor filename stem in 'actors/' (e.g., 'customer' for 'actors/customer.actor.json'). Independent of the actor display name. Cross-reference validity is enforced by core.Model.Validate() after assembly.

uml_comment.description: Optional UML comment for diagram annotations. This text appears as a note attached to the class in generated UML diagrams. Use for diagram-specific clarifications that don't belong in the main description.

attributes.description: Ordered list of class attributes; array order is declaration order and matches the core model. Each attribute key must be unique within the class and is referenced by indexes and other parts of the model. If validation fails: check that each attribute has valid 'key' and 'name' fields.

key.description: Unique attribute identity subkey within the class (e.g., 'order_date', 'total_amount'). Independent of the display 'name' field. Referenced by indexes. Must match core identity pattern ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$.

name.description: The display name of the attribute (e.g., 'Order Date', 'Total Amount'). This is the human-readable name shown in diagrams and documentation. Must be non-empty. If validation fails: ensure this is a string with at least one character.

data_type_rules.description: Rules defining the data type and constraints of the attribute. Parsed atomic kinds include unconstrained, datetime (TLA+ type_spec must be Nat), span, enumeration, reference, and object. Collections use ordered, unordered, queue, or stack with an element type that may be atomic, a record ({ field: type ; ... }), or a nested collection. Free-text descriptions are also accepted when they do not parse as a formal rule.

details.description: Optional detailed description of the attribute. Explain the attribute's purpose, business meaning, and any rules governing its values. Include examples if helpful.

derivation_policy.description: Policy for deriving the attribute value if it's computed rather than stored. Contains a logic specification describing the formula or logic used to calculate this attribute.

type.description: Required. The logic kind. Must be 'value' for derivation policies.

description.description: Description of the derivation formula or logic.

notation.description: Required. The formal notation system. Currently the only supported value is 'tla_plus'. Must always be present. If validation fails: ensure this is exactly 'tla_plus'.

specification.description: Optional formal specification in the given notation.

nullable.description: Whether the attribute can be null/empty. Set to true if the attribute is optional and may have no value. Default behavior (when omitted) depends on implementation. If validation fails: ensure this is a boolean (true or false), not a string.

uml_comment.description: Optional UML comment for diagram annotations specific to this attribute. Appears as a note in generated UML diagrams.

type_spec.description: Optional type specification for the attribute's data type in TLA+ notation. Provides a precise formal type for the attribute (e.g., 'Nat', 'SUBSET STRING', 'Int'). Only meaningful when data_type_rules can be parsed into a data type.

indexes.description: List of indexes for the class. Each index is an array of attribute identity subkeys. Index membership is not validated at wire-format read time; invalid references surface via core.Model.Validate() after assembly when applicable.

items.description: An attribute key that must exist in this class's 'attributes' array. If validation fails: check spelling and ensure the attribute is defined above.

unfinished_notes.description: Optional scratch notes not yet placed in final requirement locations.
